rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF CHRISTOPHER ITHURRALDE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/30/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: It appears that the last CIU report was done belatedly due to Covid, so a current CIU report is now needed. In addition, the original statements were not yet in the confidential file. Prior to the continued hearing date, proof of some of the funds in the Wells Fargo bank account having been moved to other banks must be filed. Neither the Bank of America nor the Chase account statements clearly indicate that they are blocked. Under what authority did the conservator deposit the settlement payment into a blocked account? The incorrect remote appearance information was attached to the Notice of Hearing. Whether or not the Judicial Council forms are used by a standard account filer, a subsequent account must state the date of any order authorizing payment of fiduciary or attorney fees. [CRC 7.575(e)(6)(C).] What "furniture" was purchased on 11/2/20? Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 1//20/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/30/23, although no courtesy copy of the "Declaration" by the CPA was provided to the assigned examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and 14.59 E.] The attorney was instructed on 7/22/22 to have his client, the Court-appointed FIDUCIARY, file a verified supplement submitting the information provided to him by the CPA. If the supplemental accounting and receipts for final distribution of the reserve (tax refund) are not filed in time to deliver courtesy copies to the assigned examiner by 1/20/23, Counsel should contact the examiner to explain the delay or appear on 1/30/23.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MOJO TUBUTU
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/23/23. The amended petition, while file stamped 10/19/22, was not actually e-filed until 10/21/22 due to a shortage of court clerks. In other words, it was not yet viewable until 10/21/22. Mailing notice before the actual filing (e-filing) of the pleading requiring notice is not sufficient notice. Furthermore, the proof of service filed on 10/19/22 was incomplete at item 3 (method of service). Under what authority did the administrator hire her nephew to assist with the sale of the Lake County property? A supplement to the amended petition must be filed to address this and also why hiring the nephew is in the best interests of the estate and beneficiaries and not a conflict. Are there no other, more qualified or less expensive agents? Did the administrator serve Notice of Proposed Action? Courtesy copies of the correct proof of service and the supplement to the amended petition (any other papers filed hereafter) must be received by the assigned examiner no later than 1/13/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF LEONISA A. TRIAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing A First And Final Account
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court to determine why the first and final accounting for the period 7/2/20 - 1/6/22 and supplement accounting for the period following the conservatee's date of death has not been filed yet. [Probate Code Section 2620.] NB: The only I&A filed in this matter shows the date of appointment of the co-conservators as 7/18/20; they were appointed on 7/2/20, although they did not obtain their Letters until 7/8/20. Furthermore, that I&A shows just one cash account at US Bank "held by Leonisa A. Trias through [C]onservator Lauro A. Trias", so it is unclear if this was her account or the conservatorship estate account. No original bank statement has ever been filed to verify the assets on hand immediately preceding the date of the co-conservators' appointment. [Probate Code Section 2620(c)(2).] Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: Meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING MUST CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROSE OSTBY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/23/23. No earlier available date. No courtesy copy of the declaration filed on 11/7/22 has been received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.] The proposed personal representative still has not verified the declaration filed by his attorney on 1/25/22. [CRC 7.103(c).] No proof of service of 15 days' notice to the French consulate has been filed yet. Courtesy copies the declaration filed on 11/7/22 and any other papers filed to address these issues (the petitioner's verification of his attorney's declaration, proof of service of 15 days' notice to the French consulate), must be received by the assigned examiner no later than 1/13/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE BARBARA SHANLEY 1996 IRREVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Accepting Resignation Of Trustee And For Appointment Of Successor Co-Trustees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/9/2023 for counsel to address the following: (1) Notice of hearing needs to be given at least 30-days prior to the hearing. The notice filed on 11/2/22 does not show 30-day service requirement met. (2) Need supporting terms under which the court can approve reimbursement to Melissa Shanley from the Trust while trust accounting is not before the court. If such relief is being request is sought, further detail information must be provided for court's review and determination. (3) Caption does not set forth request for reimbursement. CRC 7.50. Notice does not set forth such request. (4) Need Proposed Order to be updated accordingly prior to hearing. Also, ex-parte application for consolidation has not been finalized. All necessary information to be filed by two weeks prior.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MYRTLE KING
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for counsels to address the following issues: PETITION Procedural Issues: 1. Need 15-day proof of service of Notice of Petition to Administer Estate [DE-121] to Yvonne King and William King III who were served with Notice of Hearing (for remote appearance) not DE-121 form. Prob. Code Sec. 8100. 2. Need explanation who Shyla Anne Feliciano and Kelly King. If they are entitled to the above #1 notice [DE-121], proof of service must be filed. Objection Procedural Issues: 1. Need information whether Objector will file a competing petition. Objection Petition #22 requests a PPF to be appointed; however, no applicable petition is before the court. 2. Need proof of service of Objection to Mr. Ibarra as Proof of service filed 10/12/22 shows service was made to another attorney Erica Rosenblatt. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.
